content.full_text:
Twombly & Associates
Governmental Relations

(916) 448-9169
(916) 443-6917
(916) 447-8336 Fax

The Ramond Building
1001 6th Street
Suite 501
Sacramento, CA 95814

March 27, 1990

Katherine Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission

Sacramento, CA 95818

Dear Ms. Donovan:

My lobbyng firm regularly provides lobbying services on a pro bono basis to
coalitions and associations. The firm receives no fee, retainer or expenses from these
associations and coalitions. Does my lobbying firm have an obligation to register or
report activities in relation to these pro bono "clients?"

Pursuant to my conversation with Linda Cassidy, I would like to confirm the
following:

A lobbying firm which provides lobbying services at no cost to an association
or coalition is not required to list such associations or coalitions on Form 601 or
report any activities in relation to the association or coalition on a regular basis.

An association, coalition or other entity which receives lobbying services from
a lobbying firm at no cost to itself is not required to report its activities in relation to
the lobbying firm providing free services.

Thank you for your written response.

Sincerely,

Michael Twombly
Twombly & Associates

[The image appears to be a mostly blank or very faint document. No clearly legible text can be discerned from this image.]
